channel.title: The Git Reporter
channel.description: A code-native newspaper of meaningful software change.

item.title: Agent Safety Gates Catch Edge Cases
item.description: Gemini CLI, OpenClaw, and Hermes all turned ordinary boundary mistakes into explicit control failures.

item.title: Kshitij Kept Hermes Approvals In Their Thread
item.description: A public Hermes Agent patch by kshitijk4poor removed a process-global session key so command approvals stay attached to the originating session.

item.title: Mistral Vibe Makes ACP Compatibility A Release Contract
item.description: Mistral Vibe 2.18.0 turns ACP behavior, editor setup, auth cleanup, and fork-session limits into release-visible evidence.

item.title: Kieran Klukas Made Crush Share Its Config Safely
item.description: Three public Crush commits by Kieran Klukas turned shared config, OAuth refresh, and malformed tool-call state into tested failure boundaries.

item.title: OpenWork Puts Self-Hosting On The Checklist
item.description: OpenWork's June 24 code changes make deployment configuration, readiness, packaging, and user-flow proof visible as project maturity work.

item.title: Fenglanhua Made OpenClaw Prove Its Memory Migration
item.description: A public bug report turned an OpenClaw memory-store regression into a doctor-owned migration with tests, vector-row restoration, and explicit proof limits.

item.title: Model Catalogs Get Plain-English Receipts
item.description: LangChain and Pi are making model/provider changes easier to inspect before they become hidden assumptions inside agent runtimes.

item.title: Shoubhit Dash Made OpenCode Read MCP Resources
item.description: Two OpenCode commits by Shoubhit Dash turned MCP resources from connected-server metadata into tools an agent can list, template, and read with limits.

item.title: Agent Control Hints Become Runtime Contracts
item.description: Fresh OpenClaw, Codex, and Hermes changes show agent projects turning speed modes, chat metadata, and worker instructions into explicit runtime surfaces.

item.title: Google Antigravity After the Transition
item.description: The public record after the June 4 transition points to Antigravity as the packaged migration and operator surface, while Gemini CLI still carries open implementation repair.

item.title: Agent Tools Get Back-Pressure Surfaces
item.description: Fresh Gemini CLI and OpenWork patches show agent products turning hangs, publish failures, voice quotas, and provider outages into explicit runtime behavior.

item.title: Ramón Medrano Llamas Let Gemini CLI Breathe
item.description: A Gemini CLI contributor fixed a scheduler starvation path by changing how parallel tool waves yield back to the event loop.

item.title: Agent Runtimes Are Learning When To Stop
item.description: Fresh OpenCode, Codex, OpenClaw, and Hermes Agent patches show agent tools turning long-running work into bounded, traceable runtime state.

item.title: Kit Langton Made OpenCode Stop With A Summary
item.description: A public OpenCode patch by Kit Langton turns an agent's configured step limit into a user-facing summary moment instead of a silent loop cap.

item.title: Agent Tools Are Getting Capability Switches
item.description: Fresh Codex, Hermes Agent, and Pi patches show agent projects narrowing when external skills, MCP tools, and package surfaces become visible.

item.title: Fred Schott Split Pi's Agent Package Surface
item.description: A public Pi commit by Fred K. Schott added base entrypoints so developers can import a smaller agent or AI surface instead of the whole package.

item.title: Agent Control Panels Move Into The Terminal
item.description: Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, and Codex all moved user-facing control surfaces closer to the agent runtime, from billing and provider setup to structured MCP prompts.

item.title: Jesse Merhi Rebuilt OpenClaw's Command Approval Path
item.description: A public OpenClaw commit by Jesse Merhi replaced command-approval parsing with a Tree-sitter-backed planner so shell decisions can travel through node and gateway execution.

item.title: Mistral Vibe Turns Release Claims Into Trust Boundaries
item.description: The v2.16 release line says workspace trust, resume speed, diff rendering, SSE parsing, and backend reasoning behavior are no longer only UX claims; several now have code and tests behind them.

item.title: The Crush Patch That Isolated Agent Shells
item.description: Kieran Klukas changed how Crush runs child processes so shell jobs cannot seize the parent session or corrupt the terminal UI.

item.title: OpenWork Turns OpenCode Into A Team Workbench
item.description: The OpenCode-powered project is building a local-first desktop and server layer so agent work can be run, shared, permissioned, and audited from one product surface.

item.title: Benjamin Shafii Put OpenWork's Docs Inside The Agent
item.description: A June 15 OpenWork patch turns product documentation into agent tools, making app help a runtime behavior instead of a repo archaeology task.

item.title: Agent Tools Are Moving Safety Into Boundaries
item.description: Gemini CLI, LangChain, and Codex landed fresh patches that put agent safety in filesystem checks, type gates, pending-tool waits, and permission-path parsing.

item.title: Om Patel's Gemini Patch Kept Skills In Their Folder
item.description: A June 15 Gemini CLI fix turns a convenience feature, installing community skills, into a tested filesystem boundary.

item.title: Agent Clients Are Learning Where Work Belongs
item.description: Fresh Codex, OpenCode, and Pi patches show agent interfaces routing threads, draft sessions, and terminal setup by local context before the user acts.

item.title: Vegard Stikbakke's Pi Patch Read the Terminal First
item.description: A June 14 Pi contribution makes first-run setup ask the terminal what background it is actually using before choosing a light or dark theme.

item.title: Agent Sessions Are Getting Recovery Paths
item.description: Fresh OpenClaw, OpenCode, Hermes Agent, and Codex patches show agent tools treating interruption and recovery as runtime work, not edge-case cleanup.

item.title: The OpenClaw Patch That Made Reapproval Visible
item.description: Jason (Json)'s June 13 OpenClaw patch turns pending node reapproval from hidden gateway state into CLI guidance an operator can act on.

item.title: Agent Context Gets Filesystem Boundaries
item.description: Fresh Crush, Codex, OpenClaw, and OpenCode changes show agent runtimes separating user memory, project context, and sandbox paths before work starts.

item.title: Mistral Vibe's Contributor Door Is Half Open
item.description: Mistral Vibe looks mature in release machinery and public feedback channels, but its own contributor guide keeps code contributions outside the main door for now.
